notes: Initial model: keep+retention+z_score+novelty+cognitive_load+net_novelty+vz_score
Replacing retention with retention_per_sec hurts — rps has negative weight alone
retention + retention_per_sec together: rps captures duration confound while retention keeps raw signal
Midpoint retention from retentionCurve. r=+0.29 vs log_views. Best individual curve signal
Retention curve variance. r=-0.32 vs log_views (lower variance = more views)
Average of last 5 curve points. r=+0.30 vs log_views
All slope signals collinear with retention already in model — negative delta
r=+0.84, r_partial=+0.82. STRONGEST new signal. Log10 of first-7-day view sum predicts total views even after controlling for keep. Early algorithmic push is the mechanism.
r=+0.41, r_partial=+0.36. Counter-intuitive: BIGGER max retention drops = MORE views. Dramatic narrative turns drive virality, not smooth retention.
r=-0.32, r_partial=-0.26. LOWER retention variation = more views. Smooth, consistent watch patterns get algorithmic reward.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.25. Retention at 75% mark predicts views beyond keep. Deep viewer engagement matters independently.
r=+0.38, r_partial=+0.24. 10-second retention mark predicts views. First 10s = algorithmic fate.
r=+0.29, r_partial=+0.24. Midpoint retention predicts views. Videos maintaining middle engagement outperform.
r=+0.29, r_partial=+0.21. (retention-70)*duration = total watch-minutes above baseline. Algorithm rewards watch time volume.
r=+0.22, r_partial=+0.21. Higher sub retention % predicts views. Even loyal audience engagement matters.
r=+0.28, r_partial=+0.20. Non-subscriber retention predicts views. Cold traffic retention = virality signal.
r=-0.30, r_partial=-0.19. Lower like/view ratio = MORE views. Viral videos reach beyond core audience.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.18. Later peak day in first 30 days = more views. Sustained/second-wave virality indicator.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.17. Bigger 0-3s drop = more views (counter-intuitive). High initial spike height matters more than drop.
r=+0.14, r_partial=+0.15. Flatter 3-10s slope = more views. Hook retention in seconds 3-10 predicts.
r=+0.01, r_partial=+0.14. Later max drop = more views. Early max drops = hook failure.
r=+0.03, r_partial=+0.10. Subs gained per 10k views weakly predicts. Quality signal to algorithm.
r=-0.38, r_partial=-0.37. Viral videos DON'T peak day 1. Lower day-1 share = more total views. Algorithmic sustained push signal.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.31. Day7/Day1 view ratio predicts total views. Sustained momentum is the key to virality.
r=-0.39, r_partial=-0.28. Counter-intuitive: lower hook hold ratio = more views. Big initial spike height (replays) matters more than keeping it.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.26. Retention at 75%/25% predicts views. The narrative middle separates viral from average.
r=+0.11, r_partial=+0.23. Rougher retention curves = more views. Dramatic pacing creates engagement through variation.
r=+0.35, r_partial=+0.23. Curve area * duration = total watch seconds per viewer. Algorithm rewards watch time volume.
r=+0.28, r_partial=+0.22. Second week vs first week view ratio. Second-wave videos outperform.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.19. Higher initial retention spike (>1.0 = replays) predicts views. Replay behavior = strong hook.
r=-0.30, r_partial=-0.19. Lower weighted engagement per view = more views. Virality dilutes per-view engagement as audience broadens.
r=+0.18, r_partial=+0.18. Slower exponential retention decay predicts views. Captures overall curve shape quality.
r=+0.34, r_partial=+0.32. body_hold*10 + d7d1 - retVar*5. Best multi-signal combo found, captures 3 independent dimensions.
46-60s = sweet spot (28/98 hit 10M+, 2.9M median). 15-30s = death zone (0/11, 513K median). 121s+ small sample but high avg.
Best content combo: vz_type=B + z_type=C. Median 8.2M (3x avg). Visual curiosity gap + challenge/outcome text framing.
Higher swipe rate = more views (26% high-view vs 12% low-view). But r_partial=0.001 — pure consequence of reaching broader audiences.
r_partial=-0.27. Lower RPM = more views after controlling keep. Viral videos get pushed to less-monetizable audiences.
medium_decay=2.8M median vs fast_decay=413K. 7x difference. Curve shape alone separates winners.
'Making' = $24M avg (23 videos). Build/creation content is strongest format. 'Shoes' $11.5M, 'testing' $14M.
LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.21 vs views but hurts regression (noise). r=-0.02 vs keep
LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.30 vs keep but too noisy for regression
LLM vision score 0/1. 80% have faces. r=+0.12 vs views but hurts model
Duration buckets don't help. Only 10 shorts in dataset. Long videos R²=0.50 in stratified model
Non-linear keep effect (keep²). Captures accelerating returns at high keep rates
keep×vz interaction. Individually kept but doesn't add on top of keep_sq (+0.001)
log10(duration). Below threshold (+0.009)
keep+retention-100. Perfectly collinear with existing features
r=+0.39, r_partial=+0.38. TOP SIGNAL. StdDev of retention curve derivative = 'dramatic pacing'. Varied rate-of-change beats monotonic decay.
r=-0.11, r_partial=-0.24. More negative mean derivative = more views. Viral videos decay faster because they start higher.
r=+0.02, r_partial=-0.18. Viral videos still losing viewers at end, not flattening. Tension maintained through finale.
r=+0.19, r_partial=+0.18. More transcript sentences = more views. Narrative density matters.
r=-0.23, r_partial=-0.15. No recovery needed after cliff. Committed audience stays regardless.
r=+0.19, r_partial=+0.12. More words = more views. Content density signal.
Biggest single-point retention drop in curve. r=+0.41 vs log_views. Signals initial virality spike
Mean abs 2nd derivative. r=+0.12. Collinear with max_cliff — doesn't add on top
75th percentile retention. Doesn't add >0.01 on top of max_cliff
Tail std dev. Doesn't add incrementally
Early/late retention ratio. Individually kept but collinear with max_cliff
Drop from peak to first minimum. Below threshold
r_partial=+0.42. BEST SINGLE RATIO. deriv_std/retentionVariation = 'dramatic pacing efficiency'. Sharp turns at specific moments + smooth overall arc.
r_partial=+0.40. body_hold × (1-d1_share). Need BOTH mid-video retention AND sustained trajectory.
r_partial=+0.38. keep × deriv_std. Hook + dramatic pacing interaction outperforms either alone.
r=0.61, r²=0.37. z(keep)+z(deriv_std)+z(d7d1) explains 37% of view variance. Best 3-signal model found.
Non-linear: <75% keep = 3-12% hit 10M+. 75-80% = 25%. 80-85% = 41%. Marginal keep above 75 doubles hit rate.
r_partial=+0.32. vz_score × deriv_std. Visual curiosity + pacing = pre-production predictable virality.
Non-linear midpoint retention effect. Retained incrementally on top of sub_ret_gap
Subscriber vs non-subscriber retention gap. r=-0.22 vs views. Broad appeal signal
8 deep curve features tested. None improve over max_cliff. Curve signal already captured
Most polynomial/interaction terms below threshold or hurt model
Uppercase ratio in title. Holdout +0.024 but CV drops — unstable/noise
17 text features tested. None stable in CV. Best individual: title_caps_ratio
OUTCOME variable (engagedViews/totalViews). +0.03 but circular — engagement predicts views
9 growth features. Most hurt model. age_days r=-0.06 (no age confound)
5-fold CV mean=0.34±0.16. Stable estimate of true generalization. Holdout R² optimistic
CV-guided backward elimination: dropped z_score, retention, vz_score, novelty, net_novelty. CV 0.340→0.361
Final: keep, retention_per_sec, cognitive_load, ret_mid, keep_sq, max_cliff, sub_ret_gap, ret_mid_sq. Holdout=0.566, CV=0.361
Piecewise linear split on keep=75. Holdout +0.06 but CV worse and higher variance
CV best piecewise at keep=70 threshold: CV=0.388±0.155. Marginal but interesting
KNN regression doesn't beat OLS. Best k=7: holdout 0.538
OLS+KNN(k=7) blend w=0.7: CV=0.383±0.140. Best CV so far but requires storing training data
Retention curve clustering (k=2,3,4). No improvement over linear features
Words/sec in hook segment. CV stable +0.014. Visual hooks beat talky hooks (weight=-0.058)
9 other segment features tested. None improve model
6 creative features. keep_odds borderline (+0.010) but doesn't pass strict threshold
Interaction and discrete curve terms. Holdout +0.01 but CV drops. Overfitting
Multi-seed (10) 5-fold CV: 0.339±0.015. Stable performance estimate for v7
At threshold=6.7: accuracy=0.85, precision=0.60, recall=0.75. Useful for screening
Q1(small): 3.6x error, Q2-Q4(medium): 1.6-2.0x, Q5(huge): 3.3x. Model best at medium range
LLM-scored hook open_loop and specificity. Neither helps. LLM content scores too noisy for n=200
14 regex patterns tested. "indestructible" stands out massively
Title contains indestructible/bulletproof/invincible/unbreakable. CV +0.036 (10/10 seeds). 7 videos avg 61M views vs 5.6M rest
LLM categorized videos into 10 concepts. INDESTRUCTIBLE avg 26.7M, SUPERHERO_BUILD avg 10.2M
SUPERHERO_BUILD category flag. CV +0.021, wins 10/10 seeds. 30 videos avg 10.2M
BEST MODEL: 11 features, CV=0.401±0.019. From 0.339 baseline to 0.401 via concept categories
Dropped ret_mid and cognitive_load from v9. CV improved 0.401→0.405. 9 features optimal
Tested signals from loop_b (deriv_std, body_hold, pat_making, pat_shoes). None improve v9
FINAL: 9 features, 10-seed CV R²=0.405. From baseline 0.147→0.405 (+175% improvement). Ran 40+ experiments, tested 100+ features
Upload day of week. Saturday avg 14M views but CV Δ < 0.01
Signal-processing features from retentionCurve FFT. All hurt — curve info already captured by max_cliff
Leave-One-Out CV: R²=0.430, RMSE=0.537 (3.4x). Most precise generalization estimate
Previous video's log10 views. Channel momentum effect. CV +0.034, 10-seed. Algorithm gives bigger push after a hit
Rolling 3-video average. Less effective than single prev_views
CURRENT BEST: 10 features, CV=0.446±0.018, holdout=0.623, multiplier=2.9x
Previous video's keep rate. Audience priming effect. Weight=-0.029 (lower prev keep = MORE views — rebound effect?)
CURRENT BEST: 11 features, CV=0.467±0.019, multiplier=3.1x. +217% from baseline
Non-linear channel momentum (prev_views²). CV +0.014, 10/10 seeds. Accelerating returns from mega-hits
CURRENT BEST: 12 features, CV=0.481±0.018. From baseline 0.147→0.481 (+227%). Three dimensions: quality+concept+momentum
Indestructible concept × previous keep rate interaction. CV +0.026, 10/10 seeds. RELEASE STRATEGY: indestructible after high-keep = super-viral
MILESTONE: CV R²>0.50! 13 features, CV=0.506±0.017, holdout=0.702, multiplier=2.6x. From 0.147→0.506 (+244%)
r=+0.37, r_partial=+0.34. Day3-7 avg / Day1 views. Algorithmic acceleration ratio. OUTLIER: 30.4x top10 vs 0.7x bottom10.
r=+0.55 (370), r_partial=+0.29 (203). Retention late/early area ratio. Videos holding late viewers massively outperform.
r_partial=-0.49 (370), -0.27 (203). Counter-intuitive: lower ret@3s/peak = MORE views. Big initial spike (replays) matters.
r_partial=+0.48 (370), +0.29 (203). Shannon entropy of retention curve. Information-rich curves outperform monotonic decay.
r_partial=+0.48 (370), +0.27 (203). Excess kurtosis of retention curve. Top10 avg=5.17 vs bottom10=2.10 (2.47x ratio).
r=-0.37, r_partial=-0.34. Viral videos DON'T follow exponential decay. Lower R² of log-linear fit = more views.
r=+0.46, r_partial=+0.43. BEST NEW COMBO: derivStd × bodyHold × (1-decayR2). Three independent dimensions combined.
r=+0.46, r_partial=+0.40. prev_views × maxCliff interaction. Channel momentum amplifies dramatic content.
r=+0.42, r_partial=+0.38. lateEarlyRatio × derivStd. Late retention quality × dramatic pacing interaction.
r=+0.23, r_partial=+0.20. Post-peak trough / peak daily views. Sustained viewership after spike.
r=+0.31, r_partial=+0.22. Watch minutes per view on Day 1. Early engagement quality before algorithm kicks in.
r_partial=-0.23 (370), -0.12 (203). Fewer retention curve inflection points = smoother narrative = more views.
TOP vs BOTTOM: viewAccel 43x, kurtosis 2.5x, maxCliff 1.7x, subViewShare 0.09x, duration 1.6x. Acceleration is king differentiator.
No text overlay in first frame predicts more views. r=-0.05 but stable CV +0.029. Counter-intuitive: clean thumbnails may perform better
Duration × retention = total watch time proxy. r=+0.35, CV +0.018. Captures watch time volume
Retention curve area × duration. r=+0.34, CV +0.010. Absolute watch seconds per viewer
OUTCOME: log10 first-7-day views. r=+0.69, CV +0.087. Strongest outcome signal — early algorithmic push
OUTCOME: revenue per mille. r=-0.01, CV +0.033. Lower RPM = broader (less monetizable) audience reach
OUTCOME: likes per 10k views. r=-0.29, CV +0.041. Inverse: viral videos dilute per-view engagement
OUTCOME: day7/day3 view ratio. r=+0.36, CV +0.032. Sustained growth trajectory signal
Forward selection: v13 + has_text_overlay + duration_x_retention. Predictive-only CV=0.384
All signals (13+6): CV=0.661, holdout=0.739. Massive gain from outcome variables but circular
0/27 spike_decay videos hit 10M+. Day1 share >15% = DEATH. Median 0.3M. Anti-viral trajectory.
r_partial=+0.36. Delayed peak (day 3+) shape: 31% hit 10M+, median 3.6M. Best trajectory category.
r=-0.52, r_partial=-0.50. Day1 views as fraction of 30-day total. STRONGEST trajectory signal after controlling for keep.
r_partial=-0.34. How well does exponential decay fit? Lower R² = more views. Viral = non-predictable trajectory.
Controlling for keep+maxCliff: viewAccel +0.32, narrativeMomentum +0.31, decayR2 -0.30, entropy +0.20. These 4 truly independent.
r=+0.32, r_partial=+0.19. Hook replay intensity (peak retention above 1.0). Top videos have 1.4+ replay peaks.
r=+0.30, r_partial=+0.25. How far through video before 70% retention threshold. Threshold-based narrative quality signal.
r=-0.26, r_partial=-0.19. Lower StdDev in middle 50% of curve = smoother narrative = more views.
r=0.632. BEST 3 NEW SIGNALS: z(kurtosis)+z(viewAccel)-z(day1share). Explains 40% of view variance from new discoveries alone.
r=0.622. keep + day1share = 39% view variance from just 2 signals. Day1share captures trajectory independently of quality.
r_partial=-0.27 (370). FEWER extreme derivative points = more views. Viral pacing is EVEN, not wildly variable.
r_partial=+0.20 (370). Flat middle section (low variance segments 3-7) predicts views. Consistent narrative holding.
subView<5%: 81% hit 10M+ (19.3M avg). subView<10%: 45% hit rate. Algorithm reach is THE differentiator (but outcome).
keep>=75 AND maxCliff>=0.15: 91/203 videos (45%), 36% hit 10M+, avg 5.2M. Practical pre-screening formula.
maxCliff>=0.25: 15 videos, 40% hit 10M+, avg 5.7M. Extreme dramatic moments are the power zone.
Engaged%: 92.5% for 5-10M tier vs 56.5% for 100M+. Viral videos reach casual viewers. Swipe 43.5% vs 7.5%.
Sub vs nonSub retention gap REVERSES by tier: 100M+ subs watch 4.2pts more, 10-50M subs watch 1pt LESS. Mega-viral engages loyal fans differently.
r=+0.24, r_partial=+0.15. More narrative transition words (but, however, then) = more views. Narrative structure signal.
r=-0.21, r_partial=-0.18. Concave cumulative view curve = growing interest over time. Viral videos have growth-oriented trajectories.
Ideal viral curve: 1.64x replay spike → 1.17 at 5% → 0.70 at 95%. Top-bottom gap GROWS toward tail (0.015 at 5% → 0.097 at 95%).
4-7 days between uploads: 33% hit 10M+. 1-3 days: only 11%. Too-fast uploading hurts. Algorithm needs breathing room.
After 10M+ hit: 33% next video also hits (avg 4.1M). After sub-1M miss: 14% hit rate (avg 1.1M). 2.4x momentum effect.
r_partial=+0.12. Using same visual Zeigarnik type as previous video slightly boosts views. Consistency of visual style helps.
r_partial=+0.18. shares/views × day7/day1 ratio. Proxy for organic viral spreading beyond algorithm.
66 deep retention curve features tested individually. All collinear with max_cliff — curve info fully captured
Slope of 5-pt moving avg retention curve. r=+0.33. Captures overall decay trend independently of max_cliff single-drop
Word count of AI-generated videoIdea. r=+0.02. Longer/richer video concepts predict slightly more views
Both signals together on v14 base. CV=0.418. Independent dimensions: curve quality + concept richness
GPT-4o-mini scored color_contrast, composition, scale, action, objects, brightness, person. r=+0.22 best but Δ<0.01 in regression
Rolling windows, streaks, topic switching. All captured by prev_views. Combined hurts model (-0.12)
Ridge regression α=0.1 best. Small boost from regularization. Suggests mild OLS overfitting
Non-linear models all underperform OLS at n=203. Best single: OLS=0.413. GBM overfits
OLS+GBM blend captures non-linearity. Best blend w=0.7/0.3. But not compatible with linear model format
GBM importance: max_cliff(0.19), dur_x_ret(0.13), keep_sq(0.10), keep(0.10), smooth_slope(0.09)
LLM stakes score × duration/60. High-stakes longer content performs better. CV +0.034
Title contains making/build/creat/construct. 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M rest. CV +0.012
LLM-scored stakes (1-10). Incremental on top of stakes_x_duration. High stakes = more views
Other LLM content scores. Concept_simplicity marginal r=+0.11. Shareability near zero r=-0.01
Transcript pacing features (word density variation, pauses). All hurt model
Dropped hook_word_density, pat_indestructible, keep, max_cliff, retention_per_sec. All captured by other features

15 features: keep_sq, sub_ret_gap, ret_mid_sq, cat_superhero, prev_views, prev_keep, prev_views_sq, indestructible_x_prev_keep, has_text_overlay, duration_x_retention, smoothed_slope, idea_length, stakes_x_duration, pat_making_v2, stakes. Holdout=0.461
GBM out-of-fold predictions as stacking feature. Hurts model — OLS already captures available variance
Day 1-3 view signals all hurt v16. Model already captures what early views measure
Interactions, composites, quality scores. All overfit at n=203
CIRCULAR: log10(estimatedRevenue). Revenue ≈ f(views). r=+0.72. Not a valid predictor
OUTCOME: Revenue per mille. r=-0.01 but captures audience demographics post-upload
OUTCOME: (likes+2*comments+3*shares)/views. Quality signal but measured after upload
CEILING: Adding circular outcome signals pushes CV to 0.83. Not meaningful for prediction
Upload dates not available in metadata. Gap defaults to 7 days for all videos
Series detection (part/episode/#) in title. r=-0.04. Too few series videos to be predictive
168 extra videos available but data quality issues: anomalous retention curves, missing keep metric, noisy features. Expansion requires data cleaning pipeline
LLM-scored hook quality (5 attributes). action_vs_talk r=+0.31 best individual but none improve v16. Consistent: LLM scores too noisy at n=203
Shannon entropy of retention curve derivatives. r=+0.43 (strongest individual correlation!). Varied pacing = more views
Time before first spoken word. r=+0.15. Visual-first hooks outperform talk-first hooks
Both marginal signals combine to pass threshold! Independent dimensions: pacing variety + visual hook priority
CV breaks 0.50! 17 features. Added deriv_entropy (pacing complexity) and hook_silence (visual hook delay). No features removed in backward elimination
Tested entropy×stakes, silence×keep, vz/z re-adds, prev_entropy/silence, superhero×prev. None pass marginal threshold. V17 at linear ceiling for n=203
Ridge regularization on v17. Minimal improvement — model not overfitting with 17 features
Best ensemble: OLS(0.8)+GBM(0.2) blend. Captures non-linearity. But incompatible with linear model format
SESSION TOTAL: v1→v17. 40+ experiments, 150+ features tested, 3 LLM scoring rounds. CV 0.147→0.500 (+240%). 5 dimensions: quality, concept, momentum, pacing, hook

r=+0.31, r_partial=+0.29. Day3/Day1 views. OUTLIER KING: top10=13.78x vs bottom10=1.16x (11.86x ratio). Biggest outlier differential found.
r=-0.16, r_partial=-0.16. Lower daily view CV = more consistent = more views. Top10=1.35 vs bot10=1.89.
r=-0.20, r_partial=-0.14. Fewer comments/10k views = more total views. Tier: 100M+=0.34, sub1M=3.92 (11.5x dilution).
r=-0.17, r_partial=-0.13. Lower sub loss/gain ratio = better quality. On 370: r=-0.22.
r=-0.10, r_partial=-0.12. Shorter hook fraction = more views. 100M+ hookDur=5s vs others=7s. Efficient hooks win.
Tier: 100M+=0.47 vs sub1M=0.30 (1.57x). Uneven narrative segment lengths signal dramatic tension at top tier.
r=+0.19 (370). 100M+ videos = 100% have question in first 10s. Table stakes for top tier. Questions create open loops.
100M+ avg 4 words vs others 6-7. Weak r=+0.06 overall but strong tier separation. Concise titles at top.
r=+0.32 (203), r=+0.505 (370). MASSIVE on full set. Near-end retention is top-tier signal. Payoff delivery metric.
r=+0.32 (203), r=+0.501 (370). Very last moments retention. Near-identical to ret_90pct. Last 5-10% differentiates viral.
r=+0.85, r_partial=+0.82. STRONGEST SIGNAL EVER FOUND. But OUTCOME. Top10=97.8% nonSub vs bot10=74.9%. Algorithm reach factor.
r=+0.33, r_partial=+0.24. Growth streak × keep interaction. Best combo from new discoveries. Sustained algo push on quality content.
OUTLIER PROFILE: day3Growth separates tiers by 11.86x (top/bottom). Next biggest: subViewShare 0.09x, subNonSubDelta 4.66x, d2d1Ratio 3.39x.
100M+ has 7 segments (avg), segDurationCV=0.47, hookDur=5s, sentenceCount=15.6. Others: 5 segs, CV=0.30, hookDur=7s, sent=4.8. Top videos have MORE complex, VARIED narrative structure.
On 370 videos: retHarmMean r=+0.47, areaAbove80 r=+0.47, retDispersion r=+0.47, ret90pct r=+0.51, ret95pct r=+0.50. All MASSIVE on expanded sample. These curve tail signals unlock at n>200.
r=-0.37, r_partial=-0.40. Watch minutes per view CV. STRONG independent signal. Consistent watch quality = algorithm reward. Not in any existing feature.
r=+0.54 (370), r_partial=+0.31 (203). End/start retention ratio = payoff delivery. MASSIVE on full set. Did the video pay off its hook promise?
delayed_peak (day3+): 31% hit 10M+, avg 3.3M, n=153. early_peak: 0% hit, avg 0.4M, n=15. TRAJECTORY SHAPE IS DESTINY.
early_peak (peak day0, d1share>15%): 0% hit 10M+ (n=15). Confirms spike_decay (loop_b_58) on larger sample. Day 1 peaking = algorithm rejection.
INDEPENDENCE: deriv2Std×day3Growth=0.04, deriv2Std×first3Share=-0.10, day3Growth×first3Share=-0.11. Three truly orthogonal signal dimensions found.
After controlling for BOTH keep AND retention: first3Share r_p=-0.33, day3Growth r_p=+0.29, deriv2Std r_p=+0.28, maxConsecGrowth r_p=+0.22, curveRange r_p=+0.22.
areaAbove80, retHarmMean, ret90pct are 85-95% correlated with each other and drop to r_p=0.07-0.11 after controlling keep+retention. COLLINEAR — choose one representative.
avgWmGrowth r=-0.20, r_partial=-0.19. Watch quality declines faster for viral videos (audience dilution). Declining watch efficiency = GOOD sign (means broader reach).
BEST INDEPENDENT 3-SIGNAL SET: z(day3Growth)-z(first3Share)+z(deriv2Std). All three r>0.28 with views after double-control, mutual r<0.15. Three dimensions: acceleration, trajectory, pacing.
Original 203 without keep features: CV=0.5267

Kept on expanded dataset
r_partial=+0.52. BEST COMPOSITE EVER. d2Std×(1-f3share)×log(d3g). Three orthogonal dimensions: pacing jerkiness × sustained trajectory × early acceleration. Beats narrative_momentum (+0.43).
r_partial=+0.50. endStartRatio×log(d3g). Payoff delivery × algorithmic acceleration. Two strong independent signals combined.
r=+0.52, r_partial=+0.42. endStartRatio×(1-f3share). Curve payoff quality × sustained trajectory. Strong on both raw and partial.
r=+0.26, r_partial=+0.36. d2Std/wmCV. Pacing dynamics normalized by watch quality consistency. Novel dimension.
r=+0.36, r_partial=+0.38. mean(curve)×(1-f3share). Simple but effective: overall retention × trajectory sustainability.
r=+0.03, r_partial=+0.20. Share/comment ratio. Content that SPREADS vs just gets discussed. Independent of keep.
r=-0.08, r_partial=+0.24. Total d2 squared of retention curve. Sign FLIPS after controlling for keep. Dynamic pacing after quality.
r=+0.01, r_partial=+0.21. Curvature in last 20% of curve. Only visible after controlling for keep. Conclusion dynamics matter.
ALL transcript timing signals (word gaps, speaking rate, pauses, breathlessness) have r_partial<0.12. Transcript timing does NOT predict views.
Titles with numbers: avg 1.1M vs 2.2M without (0.52x). r=-0.18. But r_partial=+0.02 — entirely explained by keep. Number titles have lower keep.
Titles starting with 'I': avg 1.2M vs 2.0M (0.59x). r=-0.13. But r_partial=-0.03 — mostly explained by keep. First-person framing hurts keep.
totalEngagement r_p=-0.20, engQuality r_p=-0.19. ALL per-view engagement metrics are INVERSE. Viral = passive viewers. Consistent across likes, comments, shares per view.
tripleComposite r_p=+0.508 after controlling for keep+retention+maxCliff+keepSq. SURVIVES ALL 4 model features. Captures entirely new dimension.
day3Growth alone: r_p=+0.482 after controlling keep+ret+cliff+keepSq. Strongest single NEW signal after 4-feature control. Algorithm acceleration is an independent dimension.
r=+0.57, r_p=+0.62. Sub conversions per subscriber viewer. VERY STRONG but OUTCOME. Viral videos convert non-sub audiences to subscribers efficiently.
r=+0.08, r_p=+0.17. Deviating from recent 3-video avg duration predicts MORE views. FORMAT NOVELTY helps. Counter-intuitive: consistency in length hurts.
r=+0.07, r_p=+0.16. View trend over last 3 videos (ascending momentum). Captures whether creator momentum is BUILDING vs declining.
45-60s: 28% hit 10M+, avg 2.5M, n=137. 30-45s: 16% hit, avg 1.6M, n=169. 15-30s: 2% hit, avg 0.7M, n=47. DEATH ZONE confirmed <30s.
After 4-feature control: tripleComposite +0.51, d3g +0.48, payoffEff +0.49, f3share -0.32, d2Std +0.27, esRatio +0.25. ALL survive. New signals are truly ORTHOGONAL to existing model.
subEfficiency (subs gained per sub viewer) r_p=+0.62. Highest engagement metric found. But circular: algorithm pushes to non-subs who then sub. Consequence not cause.

day3Growth is UNPREDICTABLE from all pre-upload features (max r=0.08). No content feature, curve feature, or sequence feature predicts early algorithmic acceleration. It IS the algorithm's decision signal.
first3Share IS partially predictable: prevKeep r=-0.29, keep r=-0.27, ret90 r=-0.26. Better quality (own + previous) → more sustained trajectory. Partially causal chain.
BEST 2-signal combo: -z(f3share)+z(d3g). r=0.587, r_partial=0.557 (controlling keep). Just 2 trajectory signals explain 35% of view variance.
BEST 3-signal combos (TIED): f3share+d3g+esRatio AND d2Std+f3share+d3g. Both r_partial=0.579. Three-signal model captures 34% of residual variance after keep.
z(keep)-z(f3share)+z(d3g)+z(esRatio): r=0.681. 46% of view variance from just 4 signals. Combines pre-upload quality with post-upload trajectory.
prevKeep → f3share: r=-0.29. Previous video's keep rate predicts THIS video's trajectory sustainability. Mechanism: good prior keep → algorithm gives longer runway.

Meta-finding: trajectory signals (d3g, f3share) are NOT content properties — they are ALGORITHM RESPONSES. d3g captures the algorithm's amplification decision. Cannot be used for pre-upload prediction.
r=-0.26, r_partial=-0.19. Higher deviation from exponential decay fit = fewer views. Clean exponential curves outperform messy ones. Complements inflection_count finding.
r=+0.11, r_partial=+0.31. Early like rate (likes/day1views) strongly predicts total views after controlling keep. Early engagement quality = algorithm signal. But partially outcome.

vzScore → f3share: r=+0.22. Higher visual Zeigarnik → MORE front-loaded trajectory. Strong hooks attract fast initial viewers but may not sustain. Implication: vz is a day1 signal, not a trajectory signal.
